covered_sentences: Another phantom was coming towards him.
It was tall and silent.
Scrooge could not see its face or its body because it was wearing long, black clothes and a black hood.
There was something mysterious about it.

covered_sentences: When it came near him, Scrooge was very frightened.
It did not speak or move.
Are you the Ghost of Christmas Yet To Come? he asked.
The Spirit did not answer, but its long, white hand came out from the black clothes and pointed down.

covered_sentences: Are you going to show me things from the future? Scrooge asked.
His legs were trembling a lot and so he could not follow the Ghost when it moved away.
It stopped and waited for him.
He could not see its eyes but he felt that they were looking at him.

covered_sentences: Ghost of the Future! he cried. I am very frightened of you! But I know that you want to help me so I will go with you. Please speak to me!
It made no reply. Its long hand pointed ahead.
All right, I will come, said Scrooge.

场景：深夜，黑暗虚空，勇气宣告节拍。

光影与色彩：深蓝黑背景，斯克鲁奇被正面冷白光打亮，强调勇气。

主体与角色：逐格角色预算：主画面可辨识1人——斯克鲁奇（引用C1）；圣诞将来之灵（引用C9）骨感白手在右侧边缘离画。

约束条件：角色外观、服装细节、场景家具布局严格遵循参考图，不自行添加或修改任何细节，人物外观与比例保持前后一致。 画面内不得渲染中文文字，无水印，无多余人物，人物比例自然，道具大小合理，空间关系清楚。 斯克鲁奇（吝啬时期）禁止：不要变成真实摄影风；不要出现背景元素；不要出现文字气泡水印。 圣诞节之灵·未来禁止：不要变成真实摄影风；不要出现背景元素；不要出现文字气泡水印。 英文拼写正确，无任何气泡外浮动文字、旁白说明文字、标题文字，气泡不遮主体。画面内所有英文字母统一使用手写体风格：随性手写字母，笔画粗犷有力，基线轻微起伏，字母间距自然。

covered_sentences: So the Ghost carried him to the centre of London.
At a place called the Exchange he saw a lot of businessmen.
Their pockets were full of money. They were walking around and talking to each other.
They often jingled the money in their pockets and looked at their watches.

covered_sentences: Scrooge knew many of them. When the Ghost stopped near three men, he could hear their conversation.
No, I do not know much, said one very fat man. I only know he is dead. When did he die? another man asked. Last night, I think. I thought he would never die. Was he very ill? God knows.
What about his money? asked a man with a very red face. I do not know, replied the fat man. He has not left it to me. Everybody laughed.

构图与镜头：对话双格构图，左右两格，比例1:2（左格辅格：斯克鲁奇和幽灵靠近三个商人；右格主格：三个商人的对话）。左格：镜头位于斯克鲁奇右肩后，过肩视角，中景，看向幽灵停在三个商人旁边，斯克鲁奇跟随；右格：镜头位于三个商人正面，中景，看向三人谈论的场景。比例锚点：左格斯克鲁奇（引用C1）全身约占画面高度55%，圣诞将来之灵（引用C9）黑色剪影在旁边较大；右格三个不可辨识商人约占画面高度60%，石柱建筑为后景参照。

动作与关系：左格：斯克鲁奇（引用C1）和圣诞将来之灵（引用C9）靠近三人，斯克鲁奇面露好奇；右格：三个不可辨识商人（一个非常胖的，一个红脸的，一个普通的）互相交谈，表情漠然；一个胖商人头顶对白气泡内容抽取dead（深灰色向下箭头+深蓝色问号，承载he is dead语义）；红脸商人头顶对白气泡内容抽取money（棕色钱袋+深蓝色问号，承载What about his money语义）；附B级符号：三商人旁有漠然的笑声气泡（表达Everybody laughed的讽刺）。

场景：伦敦交易所广场（引用S11），白天（未来），商人谈话节拍。

光影与色彩：冷白天光，商业区冷峻气氛。

I will go if there is a big lunch, the red-faced man said.
Another laugh. And then the men went away.

covered_sentences: Scrooge looked at the Ghost. Who are they talking about? he asked.
But the Ghost said nothing. It went into the street and showed Scrooge two men. He knew them. They were rich and important businessmen.

First they said hallo. Then one of them said: Well, he is finally dead. Yes, I have heard, answered his companion. Cold, is not it? Very cold. But it is the right weather for Christmas. Would you like to come ice-skating? No, thank you. I am too busy. Good morning.

covered_sentences: That was the end of their conversation.
Scrooge was surprised.
Who were they talking about? He could not think of anybody. Old Marley died seven years ago and this Ghost was showing him the future.

He decided to wait and see. He looked around but could not see himself anywhere. Was not he there in the future? Silent and black, the Ghost stood near him. He knew that it was watching him and he trembled.

covered_sentences: They went into a poor part of the city where the streets were dirty and narrow.
There were dark shops and houses, and the people looked ugly and miserable. A lot of them were drunk.
Rubbish was everywhere, and there were bad smells. The quarter was full of dangerous criminals.
Scrooge followed the Ghost into a small, dark shop.
It was full of dirty, old things—bottles, clothes, keys, chains. A man of about seventy with grey hair sat near a fire and smoked his pipe.

covered_sentences: His blankets? Of course! He will not need them where he is going.
Here is a beautiful, expensive shirt too. He was wearing it for his funeral. I thought, What a pity! This is a very fine shirt but nobody will wear it again. So, I took it off him.
You did well, madam, laughed Old Joe. You are a clever woman and you will make a fortune one day.

I must think of myself, like him. He was a selfish old miser. I cleaned his rooms and his clothes. I worked very hard for him but he never gave me anything. I wanted to take more things but his housekeeper took them before me.

covered_sentences: Just then the housekeeper came in. She had a large bag full of sheets, towels, clothes, and shoes.
Now look in my bag, Old Joe, she said, and tell me how much you will give me.

Old Joe counted up the money for each thing in the box and the bag and wrote some numbers on the wall. That is how much I will give you, he said. And no more. I always give too much and so I am poor. Then he opened a dirty bag and put the money on the floor.

covered_sentences: He was shaking with terror. The body was cold, rigid, and alone in that dark room. How terrible! thought Scrooge. Not a man, woman or child to say that he was kind to them in life and to remember him with love!

Spirit! he said. What a horrible place! I will always remember this scene. Can we go now?

covered_sentences: Bob came in. He drank some tea while the two little Cratchits put their faces close to his, saying, Do not be sad, Father!
So Bob tried to be cheerful; but suddenly he cried, My little child! My little boy!
He went to a room upstairs. It looked as bright and happy as Christmas.
He sat on a chair next to the bed. There was a little child on it. It was Tiny Tim, and he was not sleeping. He was dead.
Bob kissed the little face; then he went downstairs.

covered_sentences: I met Mr Scrooge nephew in the street, he told the family.
He asked me why I was so sad. When I told him, he said he was very sorry and wanted to help us.
I think he is going to find a job for Peter. He is a very good man, said Mrs Cratchit.
Yes. Children, when you all leave home in a few years, you will not forget Tiny Tim, will you? Never, Father! they all cried. Thank you. I feel happier now, Bob said.

covered_sentences: Scrooge said to the Ghost, Oh, please tell me who that dead man was!
The Ghost took him near his office, but it did not stop. Wait! said Scrooge. My office is in that house. Let me go and see what I will be in the future.
The Ghost continued walking. Scrooge ran to the window of his office and looked in. He saw an office, but it was not his. Everything was different, including the man at the desk.
He followed the Ghost again. It stopped at the gate of a cemetery.
Am I going to learn the dead man name now? asked Scrooge.

covered_sentences: The Spirit led him to a grave. He went near it, trembling. Before I look at the name, he said, answer me one question. Is it really necessary for these things to happen or are they only possible?
The Ghost did not answer. I mean, if men change their lives and become better, will the future change too? Is this what you want to tell me?
The Ghost was silent. Scrooge went slowly towards the grave, still trembling. He read the name on the gravestone: EBENEZER SCROOGE.

He fell on his knees. I was the dead man in the bed! Oh, Spirit! Oh no, no! Listen, I have changed. I will not be the same man as before. Tell me there is still hope, please! Tell me that if I change my life, the things that you have shown me will be different!

The Spirit hand trembled. I will celebrate Christmas with all my heart! Scrooge continued. And I will always try to have the Christmas spirit every day of the year! I will live in the past, the present and the future. I will not forget the lessons that they teach.
Oh, tell me that I can clean the name off this stone! Scrooge held up his hands to the Ghost but suddenly it vanished. There was only a bed-curtain in front of his eyes.
